text: UAVs have wide spread of applications in public and civil sectors. These are applicable in scenarios where the involvement of human life is undesirable. Due to multi-UAV based scenarios, numerous tasks can be carried out in an efficient, optimal and less expensive manner as compared with a single UAV system. Although there are numerous challenges and issues to be sorted out in order to implement the use cases of UAVs based context aware networks on a stable and reliable ground. Most of the works carried out in the domain of Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) and Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are not directly suitable for UAV networks. A UAV network could have slow to high dynamics, intermittent links and dynamic topography. While an ad-hoc mesh network is believed to be the most suitable topology for the UAV network architecture of multi-UAV systems is still an understudied area. Software Defined Networking (SDN) is a new network paradigm that aims to simplify network deployment and management, as well as reduce capital and operational expenditures, enhance network security, and improve network reliability. The routing requirements of a UAV network far exceed those of MANETS and VANETS. Hence there is a need for designing protocols that have high mobility, dynamic topology, intermittent links, power constraints, varying link qualities. In the context of a UAV network, network node failures are common, which results in network partitions thereby requiring protocols to work with delay and disruption tolerance. The very nature of the long endurance of UAVs and limited life span of the nodes in a network along with their dynamic nature of the network necessitates the provision of highly reliable handover mechanisms that has been experimented in the domain of MANETs and VANETs where research is still in a very nascent stage. Also energy constraints of UAV nodes need to be utilized to the maximum in prolonging the lifetime of the network and thus needs to optimize the flow of power across all layers in a network. We intend to carry out a survey of research works associated with addressing all the challenges associated with UAV networks to bring to the research community the need to carry out more and in-depth research in these domains.
headline: Fully Human Written
